name: labs-review-block
title: Labs Review Block
description: The review loop: read the panel, pick the result that moved, look at its history — with too-thin histories listed rather than trended.

props.name: panels
props.description: The grouped results, as the laboratory reported them.

props.name: histories
props.description: `{ key, analyte, unit, data, range?, significantChange? }` — keyed to the panel rows.

props.name: defaultAnalyte
props.description: Shown on load. Defaults to the first history.

props.name: minimumForTrend
props.description: Below this many results, the analyte is listed instead of plotted.

clinicalNotes: Selection lives in the block rather than the table, so the panel stays a record of what the laboratory reported and the chart stays a view of one analyte. `minimumForTrend` is the round's honesty rule made structural: an analyte with two results is listed, not plotted, because a confident line through two points is indistinguishable from a line through two hundred. The demo shows both paths at once — creatinine has six results and trends (with an assay change breaking the line), potassium has two and is listed. Nothing here classifies a result or decides what is critical; the laboratory did that before the data arrived.
notes: Layer-M4 block. Client component. Composes lab-panel-table + lab-result-chart + chart-insufficient-data.
